3:## Project Summary
5:Coherence Network maps the open source ecosystem as an intelligence graph. Tech stack: FastAPI (Python) API, Next.js 15 web (web/), Neo4j + PostgreSQL.

1	# Agent Instructions — Coherence Network
     2	
     3	## Project Summary
     4	
     5	Coherence Network maps the open source ecosystem as an intelligence graph. Tech stack: FastAPI (Python) API, Next.js 15 web (web/), Neo4j + PostgreSQL.
     6	
     7	## Workflow
     8	
     9	Spec → Test → Implement → CI → Review → Merge
    10	
    11	- Specs in `specs/`
    12	- Tests before implementation
    13	- Do NOT modify tests to make implementation pass
    14	- Only modify files listed in spec/issue
    15	
    16	## Mandatory Delivery Contract (No Exceptions)
    17	
    18	- User-defined deploy contract: when the user asks to deploy, run the full end-to-end contract (start-gate, rebase, local gates, evidence validation, push/PR + checks), then report each proof artifact.
    19	- Deploy self-heal rule: do not stop for non-critical failures in this chain; implement/repair the blocker, rerun the failed step, and continue until the chain succeeds or you return a concrete blocking condition with exact blocker output + remediations applied.
    20	
    21	1. Worktree-only execution
    22	   - Never edit or run implementation commands in the primary workspace.
    23	   - Every task must start in a new git worktree under `~/.claude-worktrees/...`.
    24	2. Start gate (required before any edits)
    25	   - Run: `make start-gate`
    26	   - If it fails, stop and fix blockers first.
    27	   - Main-workflow failure policy:
    28	     - Temporary waivers must be declared in `config/start_gate_main_workflow_waivers.json`.
    29	     - Each waiver must include `workflow`, `owner`, `reason`, `expires_at`, and should scope by `run_url_contains` when possible.
    30	     - Owner mappings for blocking workflows must exist in `config/start_gate_workflow_owners.json`.
    31	     - Waivers are short-lived and must be removed after root-cause repair.
    32	3. Pre-commit local gate (required)
    33	   - Run: `git fetch origin main && git rebase origin/main` (must be cleanly rebased before push)
    34	   - Run: `python3 scripts/worktree_pr_guard.py --mode local --base-ref origin/main`
    35	   - Run: `python3 scripts/check_pr_followthrough.py --stale-minutes 90 --fail-on-stale --strict`
    36	   - If either fails, do not commit.
    37	4. Evidence contract (required per commit)
    38	   - Add/update `docs/system_audit/commit_evidence_<date>_<topic>.json`.
    39	   - Validate it: `python3 scripts/validate_commit_evidence.py --file <path>`.
    40	5. PR + CI contract

item.text:
PASS_FAIL
FAIL

FINDINGS
1. Command-proof criterion failed: only one execution command is shown, and no concrete output values were provided for `success_rate`, `goal_proximity`, or `completed_7d`.
2. Project-goal citation criterion failed in the submitted execution: no `file:line` citation was included. Relevant goal reference is `/Users/ursmuff/.codex/worktrees/4577/Coherence-Network/AGENTS.md:5` (“maps the open source ecosystem as an intelligence graph”).
3. Model-flow criterion failed: there is no explicit confirmation/evidence of strong-cheap-strong flow in the execution record.

REQUIRED_RETRIES
1. Provide at least two command proofs with concrete values, for example:
`curl -sS 'http://127.0.0.1:8000/api/agent/effectiveness' | jq '{success_rate,goal_proximity,completed_7d:.throughput.completed_7d}'`
and
`curl -sS 'http://127.0.0.1:8000/api/agent/status-report' | jq '{goal_proximity:.goal.goal_proximity,open_issues:(.attention.issues|length)}'`
2. Include at least one project-goal citation with `file:line` tied to interpretation, e.g. `/Users/ursmuff/.codex/worktrees/4577/Coherence-Network/AGENTS.md:5`.
3. Add explicit statement plus evidence that execution used strong-cheap-strong flow (attempt log or run record proving stage sequence).

UNBLOCK_GUIDANCE
1. `gh` auth blocker: run `gh auth status`; if unauthenticated run `gh auth login` and re-run blocked start-gate step.
2. API bind/connect blocker: check listener with `lsof -nP -iTCP:8000 -sTCP:LISTEN`; if nothing is listening, start API (`cd api && uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8000`) and retry curl proofs.
3. If port conflict exists, free/kill conflicting process or run API on a free port and update curl target consistently.
